contents:
# Ten European Countries Sign Statement for Northern Navies Initiative

## Overview
Ten European countries signed a statement of intent to establish the Northern Navies Initiative, a joint naval force aimed at deterring future maritime threats. Britain's Royal Navy head, Gen. Gwyn Jenkins, announced the agreement. Participating nations include Britain, Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, the Baltic states, and the Netherlands.

## Details
The initiative focuses on creating terms for coordinated naval operations in northern waters. Gen. Jenkins highlighted the need to counter emerging maritime risks. The group represents key NATO and non-NATO Arctic-adjacent powers. This builds on existing defense cooperation amid heightened geopolitical tensions. No specific deployment timeline remained detailed in the announcement.[3]

**Location**: London, United Kingdom

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @ianbremmer, which covers Eurasia Group political risk geopolitics global. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Eurasia Group political risk geopolitics global will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
